creator: smoergrd@oslo.geco-prakla.slb.com
text:
gallatin@cs.duke.edu writes:
> o Many Pentium Pro class features in pmap.c are enabled if cpu == CPU_686.
> With the recent addition of the CPU_PII, the CPU_PII cpu needs to be 
> added to those tests. 

Hmm, I should have checked this when I introduced the code to identify
PII processors, so color me guilty. But as Poul-Henning remarked,
these tests should check the CPU class and not the CPU model, so your
fix is inadequate (or perhaps I should rather say that it is only a
short-term fix - until the next 686-class CPU hits the market)

I'll try to set aside some time to look into this. Thank you very much
for pointing it out to us.

DES (bad DES, baaaad DES)
-- 
Dag-Erling Smørgrav - smoergrd@oslo.geco-prakla.slb.com
